provenance.source_corpus: Early recruitment of boreal forest trees in hybrid poplar plantations of different densities on mine waste rock slopes

source_spans.text: Density eﬀ ects of young hybrid poplars were assessed on the emergence and survival of early, mid and late successional species
Mean dry herbaceous biomass was > 3 times lower under the 1 × 1-m spacing treatment compared to the control plots without nurse trees
The 1 × 1-m poplar cover intercepted on average ~70% of full sunlight
Summer mortality rates, on the other hand, increased linearly with hybrid poplar density in 2015
Diminution of light availability thus corresponded to increased seedling mortality rates for these species
Results were also compared in 2 × 2-m plantations with and without a hydroseeded herbaceous cover
Mean emergences in 2014 were also significantly inﬂuenced by the hydroseeded treatment: nearly 3.5 times less seedlings emerged in hydroseeded plots
The hybrid poplar spacing treatment signiﬁcantly inﬂuenced the summer emergence of P. glauca... rates increased linearly with poplar density
The hybrid poplar spacing treatment signiﬁcantly inﬂuenced the summer emergence of... A. balsamea, while rates increased linearly with poplar density
The positive eﬀ ect of density on recruitment success after one growing season was signiﬁcant only for A. balsamea

A. balsamea mortality also remained unchanged by the hybrid poplar spacing treatment, but signiﬁcant diﬀerences were observed under the hydroseeded treatment... with higher rates registered in hydroeseeded plots
linear regression coeﬃcients indicated a signiﬁcant positive eﬀ ect of poplar density on soil moisture conditions
Salicaceae seedling abundance was greater... in plots without a hydroseeded cover... with mean value as low as < 0.3·m−2 in hydroseeded plots
